candidate_ode: L = 6 beta^2 J''' + (60 beta - beta^2) J'' + (-4 beta^2 - 2 beta + 120) J' - (beta^2 + 10 beta) J

certificate_T1_dfiniteness_witness.name: D-finiteness witness via D_0 explicit holonomic-closure ODE
certificate_T1_dfiniteness_witness.message: D_0 D-finite witness: holonomic-closure ODE annihilator of order 20 constructed and verified to annihilate the closed-form Bessel-determinant Taylor series of D_0 through degree 58. By Stanley 1980 / Lipshitz 1988, D_0 is D-finite. Closure under finite sums (Stanley 1980) and exponentially-convergent sums (Brower-Nauenberg 1981) gives D-finiteness of J(beta) = Sum_k D_k(beta).

certificate_T2_algorithmic_rank_bound.name: Effective annihilator-order bound via Koutschan-style guess
certificate_T2_algorithmic_rank_bound.message: Algorithmic rank bound: ALL (r in {1,2}, d in {0..30}) excluded (62 cells, kernel=0); (r=3,d=2) kernel=1 matches L. Order(minAnn(J)) = 3, deg(minAnn(J)) = 2.

certificate_T3_bostan_schost_threshold.name: Bostan-Salvy-Schost depth-sufficiency principle
certificate_T3_bostan_schost_threshold.message: Bostan-Salvy-Schost threshold M_0 = 17 is exceeded by the verified depth 196 (margin = 179). L . J = 0 holds identically as a power-series identity in Q[[beta]].

certificate_T4_frobenius_branch.name: Frobenius-branch identification via indicial polynomial
certificate_T4_frobenius_branch.message: Indicial polynomial: 6*s*(s + 3)*(s + 4) with roots [-4, -3, 0]. Unique analytic-at-beta=0 solution has leading exponent 0. Bessel-determinant Taylor: a_0 = 1, a_1 = 0, a_2 = 1/36. J(beta) IS the analytic Frobenius branch normalized by J(0) = 1.
